growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Defined thermophile medium with glucose, yeast extract and thiosulfate (as in Part I, Boileau et al.)

up.method: derived: qS = mu/Yx_glu + m_glu = 0.9/20.9(g/mol)->43.06 mmol/gDW/h + maintenance 2.2 mmol/gDW/h = 45.3
derived: q_thio = mu/Yx_thio = 0.9 h-1 / 3.167 gDW/mmol = 0.284 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: derived: q_ac = Yact_glu * (mu/Yx_glu) = 1.38 mol/mol * 43.06 mmol glucose/gDW/h = 59.4
derived from stoichiometry H2 = 2a (a=Yact_glu=1.38): YH2_glu=2.76 mol/mol * 43.06 = 118.8
derived from stoichiometry CO2 = a = 1.38 mol/mol * 43.06 = 59.4
stoichiometric parameter b, determined by carbon balance (glucose C minus C in acetate+CO2+biomass+EPS); numeric value not tabulated in text
produced from thiosulfate reduction; rate governed by Yx_thio and mass-transfer terms, not tabulated as a specific rate

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Overall fermentation: C6H12O6 + kH2O -> a C2H4O2 (acetate) + b C3H6O3 (lactate) + 2a H2 + a CO2 + c Biomass + d EPS
mfa.note: a = Yact_glu(max) = 1.38; H2 = 2a; CO2 = a. ~5% of consumed glucose to biomass, ~22% to EPS (ref 22).
maximum experimental acetate yield; decreases ~50% as H2 rises 0->100% in gas phase
= 2a; decreases 2.76 -> 2.3 mol/mol under H2 inhibition. Cf. Mars et al. cited 2.9 mol/mol

growth_records.cite: Richard Auria et al. Hydrogen production by the hyperthermophilic bacterium Thermotoga maritima Part II: modeling and experimental approaches for hydrogen production. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2016). DOI:10.1186/s13068-016-0681-0. [PMC5168804]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum growth rate of T. maritima (mu_max = 0.7-0.9 h-1), measured in this study... mu_max was chosen at 0.9 h-1. Yx/GLU = 20.9 g biomass/mol glucose... maintenance coefficient (2.2 mmol/g/h). Y_ACT/GLU max ... equal to 1.38 mol/mol. Y
growth_records.notes: Batch fermentation of hyperthermophile T. maritima MSB8 (DSMZ 3109) on glucose; kinetic paper, not 13C-MFA, but stoichiometry and yields are experimental (obtained from batch bioreactor without fitting). EXPERIMENTAL values: mu_max 0.7-0.9 h-1 (model used 0.9), mu_death 0.05 h-1, Yx/glu 20.9 gDW/mol glucose (after subtracting maintenance), maintenance m_glu 2.2 mmol/gDW/h, Yx/thio 3.167 gDW/mmol, Yact/glu 1.38 mol/mol, YH2/glu 2.76 mol/mol, YCO2/glu 1.38 mol/mol, Ksglu,app 5.7 mmol/L, Ksyeast 0.3 g/L, Ksthio 0.052 mmol/L. DERIVED rates (arithmetic shown in fields): doubling_time=ln2/0.9=0.770 h; q_glucose=mu/Yx_glu+m=0.9/20.9*1000+2.2=45.3 mmol/gDW/h; q_thio=0.9/3.167=0.284 mmol/gDW/h; q_acetate=1.38*43.06=59.4; q_H2=2.76*43.06=118.8; q_CO2=1.38*43.06=59.4 mmol/gDW/h. CITED-from-other-work (not this strain's measurement): literature Thermotoga H2 yields 3-4 mol/mol and productivity 2.7-12.4 mmol/L h; Mars et al. [16] measured Yact/glu 1.4, YH2/glu 2.9 mol/mol; Huber et al. [20] mu 0.6 h-1; Rinker & Kelly [23] Ksglu 0.015 mmol/L (chemostat).
